title: Multi-Agent Ecosystem for Strategic Task Reasoning and Orchestration

coordinator.department: Department of Computer Science, Chair of AI Foundations

abstract: MAESTRO develops next-generation AI agent architectures that combine structured planning, persistent memory management, and robust multi-step reasoning to autonomously accomplish complex real-world tasks across the Apply AI sectors. The project advances the state of the art in three tightly integrated research pillars: (1) neuro-symbolic planning and reasoning engines that decompose high-level goals into executable sub-task graphs with verifiable intermediate states; (2) adaptive memory architectures that unify short-term working memory with external long-term knowledge stores to sustain coherent, context-aware agent behaviour over extended interactions; and (3) decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols that enable heterogeneous AI agents to negotiate, delegate, and jointly execute tasks with provable convergence guarantees. MAESTRO validates these capabilities through three domain-specific demonstrators in healthcare clinical decision support, advanced manufacturing process optimisation, and autonomous logistics planning — each co-designed with sector-leading industry partners. All results will be made available through the AI-on-demand platform and validated in Testing and Experiment Facilities, contributing directly to the Apply AI Strategy and the European Partnership on AI, Data and Robotics (ADRA).
